title: Front-half comparison and memory phase
summary: After the front-half parity pass, lane 2 should turn that richer content into better comparison and memory payoff. This wave adds a few stronger beach/scrub/forest comparison candidates and then deepens the atlas or sketchbook memory language for the new front-half material.

execution_order.why_now: The front half will be richer enough that difference and habitat-role comparison become more valuable than raw totals.
Shared carriers across beach, scrub, and coast-edge forest can now teach transitions more clearly.
Comparison content must stay science-safe and notebook-like.
Once the comparison layer grows, the new content should also feel remembered and archived.

execution_order.goal: Implement front-half atlas or sketchbook memory payoff.
execution_order.why_now: The front-half branch should leave a stronger memory trace in existing archive surfaces.
Archive or sketchbook additions need to stay calm and compact.

guardrails: Use existing comparison, close-look, atlas, and sketchbook surfaces.
Do not add a new archive panel, station shell, or route logic.
Prefer shared carriers and habitat-role contrast over unrelated trivia.
Keep front-half memory copy notebook-like and short.
For the comparison half, keep `main-201` limited to `beach-pea` and `beach-strawberry`; do not widen the allowlist further in the same pass.
Do not force `dune-lupine` into the comparison pass until the scrub-side note choice is made more stable.
For the memory half, keep `main-202` as a sketchbook-note pass for `sea-rocket` and `sword-fern`; do not reopen the atlas or station shell.
